name: Jay Clayton
abstract: Judith Theresa Colantone (October 28, 1941 – December 31, 2023), known as Jay Clayton, was an American avant-garde jazz vocalist and educator.

works.t: Music for Mallet Instruments, Voices and Organ

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Steve Reich and Musicians with World Saxophone Quartet

works.t: Music for Mallet Instruments, Voices and Organ

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: So I got Jay Clayton. So Jay, who I love and is a wonderful talent, she started, and we still do that week together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: the first person who came out with electronics in music, like Jay Clayton uses voice doublers and changes of voice and unchained this pure voice
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012
